$schema_note: Deterministic LinkedIn algorithm + scheduling + pre-publish-linter rules engine for Choir. All thresholds are numeric and implementable. Data extracted verbatim from KB primary-source files; each block cites path:line. Layer = global_library (app-wide read-only seed; identical for every org/member). Engineers load this into a rules table or evaluate it in a pure function at draft/publish time. The Interest Graph model (360Brew, 150B params) is the authoritative 2025-2026 mechanic — pre-2024 reach/follower metrics are NOT comparable.
source_authority: primary-research-synthesis (van der Blom Algorithm InSights 2025 — 1.8M posts; Meet Lea / Paul Irolla PhD synthesis Feb 2026; Manca Korelc 360Brew Jan 2026; Christopher Penn / Trust Insights Sep 2025; Agorapulse 2025; LinkedIn official). See linkedin-algorithm-2025-2026.md:234-247 Source Registry.

distribution_pipeline.note: 4 sequential gates; fail an early stage and the post dies. Drives WHY golden_hour and content_velocity matter. Source: linkedin-algorithm-2025-2026.md:89-104.

stages.name: Quality Filter
stages.what_happens: AI classifies post as spam / low-quality / clear. Checks engagement-bait patterns, AI-generated signals, policy violations.

stages.name: Initial Test
stages.what_happens: Post shown to a small slice of network + topically-similar users (~8-15% of followers). Measures early engagement signals.

stages.name: Scoring & Expansion
stages.what_happens: Scores engagement QUALITY (dwell time, comments, saves). Above threshold = expands to broader interest-matched audience.

stages.name: Extended Distribution
stages.what_happens: Top performers keep reaching new audiences up to 5 days (previously 2-3). Posts grow 3x slower but live 5 days.

ranking_factors.label: Dwell time (read time on post)
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: HIGHEST signal. 15.6% engagement rate at 61+ sec dwell vs 1.2% at 0-3 sec. Outweighs likes by 3:1+.

ranking_factors.label: Comments (especially threaded / 2nd-degree)
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: Comments are 15x more impactful than likes. 2nd-degree comments have 2.6x the impact of 1st-degree.

ranking_factors.label: Saves / Bookmarks
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: HIGH and rising. 'A like means little. A save means everything.' Saves drive future content recommendations.

ranking_factors.label: Shares / Reposts
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: HIGH. Introduces content to entirely new audiences.

ranking_factors.label: Likes / Reactions
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: LOW. Easiest action, least signal value. Still better than nothing.

ranking_factors.label: Early engagement velocity (golden hour)
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: First 60-90 minutes determine 80%+ of total reach. Speed of early engagement matters, not just volume: 20 comments in 30 min > 50 comments in 24 hr. High early velocity = expanded distribution to followers-of-followers.

ranking_factors.label: Topic / niche consistency
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: Posting on the same 2-3 topics consistently raises distribution. Algorithm builds a per-user 'creator topic graph'. Pivoting topics too often RESETS distribution baseline. Interest Graph: niche authority > network size.

ranking_factors.label: Content format multiplier
ranking_factors.weight_or_effect: Carousel/document 3.7-6.6x engagement vs text; formatted text 2.1x vs unformatted; image post 2x vs text-only. See format_performance for full hierarchy.

format_performance.notes: Still king. Swiping increases dwell time. Confirmed across multiple 2025-2026 studies.
Image posts get 2x engagement vs text-only.
Still works well with strong hooks and structure.
Subtitles non-negotiable. Short-form (<60s) critical. Native > external links.
Links in body AND in comments are suppressed.

suppression_signals.detection_rule: Post body contains a URL (http://, https://, www., or bare-domain pattern like example.com/...).

suppression_signals.penalty: ~60% reach reduction (links in comments no longer avoid the penalty — King-Comment technique deprecated)
suppression_signals.detection_rule: First-party comment (or planned pinned comment) contains a URL. Use 'DM me [keyword]' or 'Link in my profile' instead.
Phrase-match (case-insensitive) on bait patterns: 'comment YES', 'comment yes if', 'like if you agree', 'like this if', 'tag a friend who', 'comment below if you want', 'drop a YES'.

suppression_signals.penalty: Can kill post growth entirely. LinkedIn detects and penalizes.
suppression_signals.detection_rule: Route all generated comments through the anti-slop / sanitize-copy gate before posting (em-dash density, kill-list, banned phrases). AI tells = penalty risk.
suppression_signals.source: linkedin-algorithm-2025-2026.md:211; feature-map.json:168 (comment generation must pass same anti-slop gate as posts)

suppression_signals.penalty: Cannibalizes reach on BOTH posts by up to 50%
suppression_signals.detection_rule: A second post scheduled/published <24h (86400 sec) after the member's previous post on the same account. Cross-ref cadence_rules.minimum_spacing_hours.
Count '#' tokens in body. >=6 = -5% penalty. 4-5 = neutral/slight-negative (warn). 1-3 = +12.6% (optimal). 0 = misses the +12.6% lift (warn).
Edit event on a post < 3600 sec after publish. Warn the member: editing within the first hour can reset distribution.

suppression_signals.penalty: Hook/key message hidden behind 'see more' fold kills dwell time and click-through
suppression_signals.detection_rule: First visible block (before ~210 chars / first line group) lacks a complete hook+rehook. Critical content must be fully visible without clicking 'see more'. See pre_publish_linter.hook_past_fold for the numeric threshold.

suppression_signals.penalty: Up to 40% less engagement; ending with a question adds 20-40%
suppression_signals.detection_rule: No CTA in the closing block (no question mark, no save/repost/comment/follow/DM prompt). See pre_publish_linter.missing_cta.
Format == poll on a serious brand-building post. Warn (not hard block).

suppression_signals.penalty: Video reach already -300% since 2023; missing subtitles compounds loss
suppression_signals.detection_rule: Format == video and no subtitle track flagged. Warn: subtitles non-negotiable; keep <60s.

best_time_slots.note: Times are in the RECIPIENT'S / audience local timezone, not the poster's. Primary window: Tuesday-Thursday 7:30-8:30 AM = single best B2B slot. Use as scheduler defaults.

by_day.rank: Good but competing with email catch-up

platform_rules_secondary.note: Broader (less precise) windows from the platform-rules voice-DNA file. Use the scheduling SKILL table above as canonical; this is a corroborating range.

cadence_rules.value: 3-4 posts per week (sweet spot). 3x/week = optimal per algorithm research.
Daily (7x/week) can hurt quality — not recommended unless quality stays high
24+ hours between posts. Posting twice within 24h cannibalizes reach by up to 50%.
cadence_rules.enforcement: HARD BLOCK on schedule/publish if <24h since member's previous post
informational (the upside of the minimum_spacing block)

cadence_rules.value: Stick to the same 2-3 topics; pivoting too often resets distribution baseline
cadence_rules.enforcement: warn on topic drift across recent posts

golden_hour_checklist.note: Post-publish engagement routine. The poster's OWN activity signals the algorithm they're active, boosting distribution; fast comment replies invite more comments (4x weight). First 60-90 min determine 80%+ of reach. Choir fires these as a post-publish task checklist with relative-time triggers.

steps.action: Engage with 5-10 other posts in your niche.
Check back, respond to all new comments.
Continue engaging on others' posts + responding.
Final check, respond to remaining comments.

engagement_weights.note: Multipliers used to score / nudge engagement quality, e.g. in the commenting feature's quality nudge. The 4x figure is the algorithm's reward for fast/quality comment replies (scheduling/SKILL.md:64). The >15-word qualifier is NOT a verbatim KB figure — it is the feature-map's operational interpretation of the commenting strategy's quality-over-quantity principle (a meaningful, substantive comment vs a 'Great post!' one-liner). Flagged in net_new; do not present >15 words as a sourced LinkedIn metric.

actions.condition: Responding quickly to comments encourages more comments; carries 4x engagement weight.
A comment is 15x more impactful than a like.

OPERATIONAL NUDGE (feature-map interpretation, NOT a verbatim KB metric): a substantive comment (>=15 words, contains an insight, ideally a question) is treated as a 'meaningful' comment worth ~4x a one-liner. Used to gate comment quality, not as a sourced algorithm figure.
actions.source: feature-map.json:168; derived from linkedin-commenting-strategy.md:94-120 (quality-over-quantity principle) + scheduling/SKILL.md:64 (4x)

comment_quality_linter.note: Real-time linter for the AI comment-generation feature (separate from the post pre_publish_linter). Blocks low-value comments at draft time and routes output through the anti-slop gate (AI comments are penalized by the algorithm). Source: feature-map.json:168,174; linkedin-commenting-strategy.md:119-120,267-273.

checks.rule: Block / hard-warn if comment matches generic-praise patterns (case-insensitive): 'great post', 'great post!', 'love this', 'so true', 'thanks for sharing', 'well said', 'spot on' with no added insight.
checks.message: This reads as generic praise. Add a specific insight or experience — '“Great post!” comments have zero visibility.'

checks.rule: Comment word count >= 15 for a quality/expansion comment (Support-type comments are exempt — they are intentionally one-liners).
checks.message: Under 15 words. Quality comments (Expansion/Call-Out/Sidecar) need substance to earn the ~4x weight; one-liners are fine only as Support comments.

checks.rule: Quality comment should contain a question (?) OR a first-person insight ('I', 'in my experience', 'we found') OR a specific reference to the post's content.
checks.message: Add a question or a specific insight — comment for the audience, not just the author.

checks.rule: Route the generated comment through sanitize-copy (em-dash density <=1/paragraph, kill-list, banned phrases) before allowing post. AI-detectable tells risk algorithm penalty.
checks.message: Comment tripped the anti-slop gate. AI-generated comments are detected and penalized — rewrite to remove AI tells.

checks.rule: Cap at 10-15 quality comments per day (server-side). More can be flagged as spam by LinkedIn.
checks.penalty: WARN at 15, BLOCK above hard cap
checks.message: You're near the daily comment cap. LinkedIn may flag >15 comments/day as spam.

comment_quality_linter.comment_types_reference: 11 comment types (6 Alic: Personal Advice, Professional Advice, Comparisons, Motivational, Humor, Tags; 5 Browne: Support, Call Out, Expansion, Comedy, Sidecar). Sidecar (full-length post-as-comment on a 10K+ creator's <1hr-old post) is the highest-ROI type. Detail: linkedin-commenting-strategy.md:132-224.

pre_publish_linter.detection: Regex match for URLs in body: /(https?:\/\/|www\.)\S+/i OR bare-domain /\b[a-z0-9.-]+\.(com|io|co|ai|net|org|so|dev|app)(\/\S*)?\b/i.
pre_publish_linter.message: External link in your post body cuts reach ~60%. Move it to your profile or use 'DM me [keyword]'.

pre_publish_linter.detection: Same URL regex applied to the planned first-party comment / pinned comment text.
pre_publish_linter.message: Links in comments are penalized ~60% too (King-Comment no longer works). Use 'Link in my profile' or 'DM me'.

pre_publish_linter.detection: Count '#' hashtag tokens in body. 1-3 => pass (+12.6% reach). 4-5 => warn (neutral/slight-negative). >=6 => warn/block (-5%). 0 => warn (misses +12.6% lift).
pre_publish_linter.message: Use 1-3 niche hashtags (+12.6% reach). 6+ hashtags = -5% reach. You have {n}.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: Hook/key message hidden behind 'see more' fold (kills dwell time + see-more clicks)
pre_publish_linter.detection: If the post's hook + rehook is not fully contained within the first ~210 characters (the mobile 'see more' fold), or the first 210 chars do not end on a complete compelling line, flag. The hook must work before the fold.
pre_publish_linter.message: Your hook runs past the ~210-char 'see more' fold. The first line must stop the scroll before truncation — tighten the opener.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: Up to -40% engagement (CTA-less posts); a closing question adds +20-40%
pre_publish_linter.detection: Closing block (last paragraph) must contain a CTA: a question mark, OR a save/comment/repost/follow/DM prompt. Phrase set (case-insensitive): 'save this', 'save it for later', 'drop a comment', 'what's your', 'what has been your', 'agree?', 'repost', 'follow me', 'follow for', 'DM me', 'let me know'.
pre_publish_linter.message: No CTA detected. Posts without a CTA get up to 40% less engagement — end with a question or a 'Save this for later' prompt.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: AI-tell; em dash overuse is the single most reliable AI signal (algorithm penalizes AI-generated content)
pre_publish_linter.detection: For each paragraph, count em-dash chars (—, U+2014). >1 per paragraph fails. Same logic as scripts/lib/sanitize-copy.mjs em-dash-density check.
pre_publish_linter.message: Paragraph {i} has {n} em-dashes (max 1). Em dashes are the #1 AI tell — use commas, periods, or parentheses.

pre_publish_linter.detection: Case-insensitive phrase-match: 'comment YES', 'comment yes if', 'like if you agree', 'like this if', 'tag a friend who', 'comment below if', 'drop a YES', 'type YES'.
pre_publish_linter.message: Engagement bait ('Comment YES if you agree') is actively filtered and suppressed. Rewrite as a genuine question.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: Outside optimal band underperforms (top-5% posts run 1,000-1,500 chars)
pre_publish_linter.detection: Char-count the body; compare to the band for the post's format. Outside band => warn with the target range. Sentences over 12 words also underperform 20% (short-sentence preference).
pre_publish_linter.message: This {format} is {n} chars; optimal is {min}-{max}. Top-performing posts run 1,000-1,500 chars with short (<12-word) sentences.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: -50% reach (cannibalization on both posts); 24h spacing = +120% visibility
pre_publish_linter.detection: At schedule/publish time, compute seconds since the member's previous post on the same account. <86400 => block.
pre_publish_linter.message: Less than 24h since your last post. Posting twice in 24h cannibalizes reach up to 50%. Spacing 24h gives +120% visibility.

pre_publish_linter.detection: Rolling 7-day post count for the member. >4 => warn (diminishing returns). 2 posts same calendar day => hard block.
pre_publish_linter.message: You're at {n} posts this week. 3-4x/week is the sweet spot; 5+ hits diminishing returns and daily posting cannibalizes reach.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: Video reach -300% since 2023; missing subtitles compounds the loss
pre_publish_linter.detection: If format == video: require a subtitle flag and duration < 60 sec.
pre_publish_linter.message: Video reach is down ~300% since 2023. Add subtitles (non-negotiable) and keep it under 60 seconds.
Polls have low engagement and low authority — skip them for serious brand building.

pre_publish_linter.penalty: Editing within the first hour can reset distribution
pre_publish_linter.detection: If an edit is attempted < 3600 sec after publish, warn the member.
pre_publish_linter.message: Editing within the first hour can reset your post's distribution. Wait until the golden hour is over.

organic_reach_context.note: Calibration baseline so Choir analytics don't quote pre-2024 numbers. Measured across 1.8M posts.

deprecated_mechanics.status: Retired Feb 1, 2024. Features (Follow button, Featured section, LinkedIn Live) distributed to all users. Do NOT instruct members to 'enable Creator Mode'.
Deprecated. Links in comments now penalized ~60% same as body. Use profile link or DM.
